revision.text:
'''下毛野 石代'''（しもつけぬ の いわしろ、生没年不詳）は、[[飛鳥時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[貴族]]。[[下野国]]を本拠地とする[[下毛野氏]]の出。本姓は'''下毛野朝臣'''だが、[[続日本紀]]によると[[707年]]（[[慶雲]]4年）[[3月22日 (旧暦)|3月22日]]、一族の[[下毛野古麻呂|下毛野朝臣古麻呂]]が石代の姓を「下毛野朝臣」から「下毛野川内朝臣」に変更することを朝廷に上申し、許可が下りていることから、この時点で'''下毛野川内朝臣'''に改姓したものと考えられる。
== 経歴 == * [[701年]]（[[大宝]]元年）[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]] - [[文武天皇]]の命を受け、[[官人]]を代表し亡くなった[[多治比嶋]]を誄する。この時、石代の官位は[[従七位下]]であった。 * [[715年]]（[[霊亀]]元年）[[1月10日 (旧暦)|1月10日]] - [[従六位下]]を下賜される。 * [[720年]]（[[養老]]4年）[[9月29日 (旧暦)|9月29日]] - [[陸奥国]]で起きた陸奥[[按察使]][[上毛野廣人]]殺害事件に対し、この時[[左京亮]][[従五位下]]であった石代は[[元正天皇]]より征夷副将軍に任ぜられ、これを征伐する。
[[Category:毛野氏|しもつけぬ の いわしろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|しもつけぬ の いわしろ]] [[Category:奈良時代の人物|しもつけぬ の いわしろ]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史|しもつけぬ の いわしろ]] [[Category:年没|しもつけぬ の いわしろ]]

'''下毛野 石代'''（しもつけぬ の いわしろ、生没年不詳）は、[[飛鳥時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[貴族]]。[[下野国]]を本拠地とする[[下毛野氏]]の出。本姓は'''下毛野朝臣'''だが、[[続日本紀]]によると[[707年]]（[[慶雲]]4年）[[3月22日 (旧暦)|3月22日]]、一族の[[下毛野古麻呂|下毛野朝臣古麻呂]]が石代の姓を「下毛野朝臣」から「下毛野川内朝臣」に変更することを朝廷に上申し、許可が下りていることから、この時点で'''下毛野川内朝臣'''に改姓したものと考えられる。
== 経歴 == * [[701年]]（[[大宝]]元年）[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]] - [[文武天皇]]の命を受け、[[官人]]を代表し亡くなった[[多治比嶋]]を誄する。この時、石代の官位は[[従七位下]]であった。 * [[715年]]（[[霊亀]]元年）[[1月10日 (旧暦)|1月10日]] - [[従六位下]]を下賜される。 * [[720年]]（[[養老]]4年）[[9月29日 (旧暦)|9月29日]] - [[陸奥国]]で起きた陸奥[[按察使]][[上毛野廣人]]殺害事件に対し、この時[[左京亮]][[従五位下]]であった石代は[[元正天皇]]より征夷副将軍に任ぜられ、これを征伐する。
[[Category:毛野氏|しもつけぬ の いわしろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物|しもつけぬ の いわしろ]] [[Category:奈良時代の人物|しもつけぬ の いわしろ]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史|しもつけぬ の いわしろ]]

== 経歴 == * [[701年]]（[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年）[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]] - [[文武天皇]]の命を受け、[[官人]]を代表し亡くなった[[多治比嶋]]を誄する。この時、石代の官位は[[従七位下]]であった。 * [[715年]]（[[霊亀]]元年）[[1月10日 (旧暦)|1月10日]] - [[従六位下]]を下賜される。 * [[720年]]（[[養老]]4年）[[9月29日 (旧暦)|9月29日]] - [[陸奥国]]で起きた陸奥[[按察使]][[上毛野廣人]]殺害事件に対し、この時[[左京亮]][[従五位下]]であった石代は[[元正天皇]]より征夷副将軍に任ぜられ、これを征伐する。

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 下毛野 石代 | 画像 = <!-- 画像ファイル名 --> | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]]後期 - [[奈良時代]]前期 | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = 下毛野石代 → 下毛野川内石代 | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[文武天皇]] → [[元明天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|元明天皇}}]] → [[元正天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|元正天皇}}]] | 氏族 = [[下毛野氏|下毛野]][[朝臣]] → [[下毛野川内氏|下毛野川内]]朝臣 | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''下毛野 石代'''（しもつけの の いわしろ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[飛鳥時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[貴族]]。[[下野国]]を本拠地とする[[下毛野氏]]を出自とする。[[氏姓]]は下毛野[[朝臣]]のち下毛野川内朝臣。
__TOC__{{-}} == 経歴 == * [[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]] - [[文武天皇]]の命を受け、[[官人]]を代表し亡くなった[[多治比嶋]]を誄する。この時、石代の[[官位]]は[[従七位|従七位下]]であった。 * [[慶雲]]4年（[[707年]]）[[3月22日 (旧暦)|3月22日]]、一族の[[下毛野古麻呂]]が石代の氏姓を「下毛野朝臣」から「下毛野川内朝臣」に変更することを[[朝廷]]に上申し、許可される。 * [[霊亀]]元年（[[715年]]）[[1月10日 (旧暦)|1月10日]] - [[従六位|従六位上]]から[[従五位|従五位下]]に昇叙される。 * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）[[9月29日 (旧暦)|9月29日]] - [[陸奥国]]で起きた陸奥[[按察使]][[上毛野廣人]]殺害事件に対し、石代は[[元正天皇]]より征夷副将軍に任ぜられる。この時の[[官位]]は[[従五位|従五位下]]・[[京職|左京亮]]。
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけの の いわしろ}} [[Category:下毛野氏|いわしろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 下毛野 石代 | 画像 = <!-- 画像ファイル名 --> | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]]後期 - [[奈良時代]]前期 | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従五位|従五位下]]、[[京職|左京亮]] | 主君 = [[文武天皇]] → [[元明天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|元明天皇}}]] → [[元正天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|元正天皇}}]] | 氏族 = [[下毛野氏|下毛野朝臣]] → 下毛野川内朝臣 | 父母 = 不詳 | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''下毛野 石代'''（しもつけの の いわしろ、[[生没年不詳]]）は、[[飛鳥時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[貴族]]。
== 経歴 == [[文武天皇|文武朝]]の[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）[[左大臣]]・[[多治比嶋]]の[[崩御#薨去|薨去]]に際して、百官を代表してを誄を行う（この時の[[位階]]は[[従七位|従七位下]]）。[[慶雲]]4年（[[707年]]）一族の[[下毛野古麻呂]]の言上により、石代は[[下毛野氏|下毛野朝臣]]から下毛野川内朝臣に[[改姓]]した。
[[元明天皇|元明朝]]末の[[和銅]]8年（[[715年]]）[[従六位|従六位上]]から三階昇進して[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙爵]]する。[[元正天皇|元正朝]]では[[京職|左京亮]]を務めるが、[[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）[[陸奥国]]で[[蝦夷]]の反乱が発生して[[陸奥按察使]]・[[上毛野広人]]が殺害されたため、反乱鎮圧のため[[多治比県守]]が[[征夷大将軍|持節征夷将軍]]に、石代が征夷副将軍に任ぜられている。
== 官歴 == 『続日本紀』による。 * [[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]） 7月21日：見[[従七位|従七位下]] * [[慶雲]]4年（[[707年]]） 3月22日：[[下毛野氏|下毛野朝臣]]から下毛野川内朝臣に[[改姓]] * 時期不詳：[[従六位|従六位上]] * [[和銅]]8年（[[715年]]） 1月10日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（越階） * 時期不詳：[[京職|左京亮]] * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]） 9月29日：[[征夷大将軍|征夷副将軍]]
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけの の いわしろ}} [[Category:下毛野氏|いわしろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.
[[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Asuka period people]] [[Category:Nara period nobility]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:8th-century deaths]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. '''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].

In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.

{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.

revision.comment: Removing this excess
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "下毛野氏}}" to "下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.

== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.

== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].

At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.

{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.
