'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.

{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].

In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|下毛野氏" to "|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan"
revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.

== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[Asuka period]] - Early [[Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[Asuka period]] to the early [[Nara period]]. He held the rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=draft}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsuke no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-rank promotion. During the reign of [[Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[Emishi]] in [[Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人|12=draft}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=draft}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[Kōdansha]] Academic Library. <!--Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Ishishiro-->

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 下毛野石代 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[飛鳥時代]]後期 - [[奈良時代]]前期 | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従五位|従五位下]]・[[京職|左京亮]] | 主君 = [[文武天皇]]→[[元明天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|元明天皇}}]]→[[元正天皇|{{color|#FC4E6B|元正天皇}}]] | 氏族 = [[下毛野氏|下毛野]][[朝臣]]→下毛野川内朝臣 | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''下毛野 石代'''（しもつけの の いわしろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]から[[奈良時代]]にかけての[[貴族]]。[[官位]]は[[従五位|従五位下]]・[[京職|左京亮]]。
== 経歴 == [[文武天皇|文武朝]]の[[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]）[[左大臣]]・[[多治比嶋]]の薨去に際して、百官を代表して[[誄]]を行う（この時の[[位階]]は[[従七位|従七位下]]）。[[慶雲]]4年（[[707年]]）一族の[[下毛野古麻呂]]の言上により、石代は[[下毛野氏|下毛野]][[朝臣]]から下毛野川内朝臣に[[改姓]]した。
[[元明天皇|元明朝]]末の[[和銅]]8年（[[715年]]）[[従六位|従六位上]]から三階昇進して[[従五位|従五位下]]に[[叙爵]]する。[[元正天皇|元正朝]]では[[京職|左京亮]]を務めるが、[[養老]]4年（[[720年]]）[[陸奥国]]で[[蝦夷]]の反乱が発生して[[陸奥按察使]]・[[上毛野広人]]が殺害されたため、反乱鎮圧のため[[多治比縣守]]が[[征夷大将軍|持節征夷将軍]]に、石代が征夷副将軍に任ぜられている。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 * [[大宝 (日本)|大宝]]元年（[[701年]]） 7月21日：見[[従七位|従七位下]] * [[慶雲]]4年（[[707年]]） 3月22日：[[下毛野氏|下毛野朝臣]]から下毛野川内朝臣に改姓 * 時期不詳：[[従六位|従六位上]] * [[和銅]]8年（[[715年]]） 1月10日：[[従五位|従五位下]]（越階） * 時期不詳：[[京職|左京亮]] * [[養老]]4年（[[720年]]） 9月29日：[[征夷大将軍|征夷副将軍]]
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1992年
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけの の いわしろ}} [[Category:下毛野氏|いわしろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:8世紀没]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野石代]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11361051]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] - Early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[:en:Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-Rank promotion. During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|qq=}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-Rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[:en:Kōdansha|Kōdansha]] Academic Library.
{{translated page|ja|下毛野石代|version=70042223|comment=Imported full ja history}}

At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-Rank promotion. During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|qq=}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-Rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[:en:Kōdansha|Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野石代]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11361051]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Shimotsuke no Iwashiro | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = Late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] - Early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = | posthumous_name = | shinto_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | court_Rank = [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]] | ruler = [[Emperor Monmu]] → [[Empress Genmei]] → [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] | clan = {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) → Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notes = }}
'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[:en:Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-Rank promotion. During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|qq=}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-Rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[:en:Kōdansha|Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[:en:Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-Rank promotion. During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|qq=}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-Rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[:en:Kōdansha|Kōdansha]] Academic Library.
{{translated page|ja|下毛野石代|version=70042223|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]] to the early [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. He held the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[:en:Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-Rank promotion. During the reign of [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]], he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] (720), when a rebellion by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]] in [[:en:Mutsu Province|Mutsu Province]] led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|qq=}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-Rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō]] 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[:en:Kōdansha|Kōdansha]] Academic Library.
{{translated page|ja|下毛野石代|version=70042223|comment=Imported full ja history}}

At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-Rank promotion. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō}}, he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} (720), when a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}} led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|qq=}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun}} 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-Rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō}} 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[:en:Kōdansha|Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

In the fourth year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun|1=Keiun}} (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-Rank promotion. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}}, he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} (720), when a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}} led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|qq=}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun|1=Keiun}} 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-Rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[:en:Kōdansha|Kōdansha]] Academic Library.

'''Shimotsuke no Iwashiro''' (下毛野 石代) was a Japanese noble active from the late {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} to the early {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. He held the Rank of [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]].
== Career == In the first year of the [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō era]] (701), upon the death of the [[:en:Sadaijin|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Tajihi no Shima|ja|多治比嶋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Shima|qq=}}, Iwashiro delivered a eulogy on behalf of the entire court while holding the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. In the fourth year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun|1=Keiun}} (707), following a petition by his kinsman {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}}, Iwashiro’s family name was changed from {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno no Ason) to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason.
At the end of the [[Wadō era]] (715), Iwashiro was promoted from [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]] to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] by an exceptional three-Rank promotion. During the reign of {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}}, he served as [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. In the fourth year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} (720), when a rebellion by the {{ill|Emishi|en|Emishi|WD=Q932574|lt=Emishi|1=Emishi}} in {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}} led to the death of the [[Mutsu Azechi|Inspector of Mutsu]], {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|ja|上毛野広人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Hirohito|qq=}}, Iwashiro was appointed Vice Commander of the expeditionary force under General {{ill|Tajihi no Agatamori|ja|多治比縣守|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajihi no Agatamori|qq=}}.
== Court Positions == According to the ''[[Shoku Nihongi]]'': * [[Taihō (Japan)|Taihō]] 1 (701), 7th month, 21st day: Held the Rank of [[Junior Seventh Rank|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade]]. * {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun|1=Keiun}} 4 (707), 3rd month, 22nd day: Changed name from Shimotsukeno no Ason to Shimotsukeno no Kawachi no Ason. * Unknown date: Promoted to [[Junior Sixth Rank|Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade]]. * [[:en:Wadō|Wadō]] 8 (715), 1st month, 10th day: Promoted to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] (special three-Rank promotion). * Unknown date: Appointed [[Kyōshiki|Governor of the Left Capital]]. * {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō|1=Yōrō}} 4 (720), 9th month, 29th day: Appointed Vice Commander for the expedition against the Emishi.
== References == * [[Ujitani Misao]] (1992). ''Shoku Nihongi'' (Volume 1), [[:en:Kōdansha|Kōdansha]] Academic Library.
